data.title: Brookfield Property Partners Completes 2024 Annual Filings
data.description:
BROOKFIELD NEWS, March 21, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Brookfield Property Partners announced today that it has filed its 2024 annual report on Form 20-F, including its audited financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2024, with the SEC on EDGAR as well as with the Canadian securities authorities on SEDAR+. These documents are also available at bpy.brookfield.com.

Brookfield Property Partners

Brookfield Property Partners is one of the world’s premier real estate companies. We own and operate iconic properties in the world’s major markets, and our global portfolio includes office, retail, multifamily, logistics, hospitality, single-family rentals, manufactured housing, student housing and self-storage.

Brookfield Property Partners is a subsidiary of Brookfield Corporation (NYSE: BN, TSX: BN). More information is available at www.brookfield.com.

Contact:Keren Dubon

Investor Relations

Tel: 212-618-3440

Email: keren.dubon@brookfield.com

data.title: Global Business Travel Group Cuts Price of Deal for Rival CWT
data.description:
By Sabela Ojea

Global Business Travel Group said that a deal to acquire rival CWT Holdings would be valued at around $540 million from $570 million after the parties amended the terms of their agreement.

Travel-management company Global Business Travel Group--also known as Amex GBT--said the amended deal also means it would issue 50 million shares of its class A common stock at the closing of the transaction, below the 72 million originally agreed and at a price of $7.50 a share from $6 a share previously.

The company added that it has also extended the merger deadline to Dec. 31 to provide both parties with additional time to defend deal against the Justice Department.

In January, the Antitrust division of the DOJ filed a lawsuit seeking to block the transaction, saying it would harm competition for business-travel-management services.

Amex GBT and CWT Holdings are the largest and third-largest business-travel-management companies in the world, respectively, the DOJ said.

The complaint alleged that Amex GBT's transaction is an effort in consolidation of the market and acts as a respite from recent customer losses to CWT. Amex GBT was trying to avoid future business loss to CWT by buying the company, the DOJ alleged.

Write to Sabela Ojea at sabela.ojea@wsj.com

data.title: 'Quality' AI Stocks Look Like a Buy, These Strategists Say — Barrons.com
data.description:
This commentary was issued recently by money managers, research firms, and market newsletter writers and has been edited by Barron's.

How, and Why, to Buy AI

UBS House View - Daily U.S. UBS March 20: Nvidia reportedly plans to spend "hundreds of billions of dollars" on U.S.-manufactured chips and electronics over the next four years, according to CEO Jensen Huang.

At a sector level, the prospect of increased semiconductor-sector spending to build U.S. capacity alongside potentially higher talent costs and less-efficient manufacturing techniques could raise medium-term margin concerns for some investors. But while U.S.-made chips are more expensive to make, they wouldn't be subject to potential import tariffs. We note that some leading chip makers actually guided for higher margins in the past quarter.

Stepping back, and without taking any single-name views, we think the pace of performance improvements for the AI compute industry, alongside the recent correction in valuations, means the risk/reward has improved for leading AI semiconductors, in particular. Given elevated market volatility and tariff uncertainty, we recommend investors consider positioning via structured strategies and by buying the dip in quality AI stocks.

Solita Marcelli and team

Where's That Fed Put?

Sevens Report Kinsale Trading March 20: The Federal Reserve provided a slightly dovish surprise and offered investors some reason for optimism, although it isn't enough to offset the headwinds still hitting stocks.

The biggest positive from the Fed statement [released after the March 18-19 FOMC meeting] was that the Fed signaled it is watching the economy closely and has detected a loss of momentum. If that continues, the Fed will act to support the economy and markets via rate cuts or other means. Practically, that means the market does have the Fed to help support it, if investors' fears are realized and the economy slows due to policy-driven uncertainty.

Negatively, the Fed doesn't appear in a big hurry to help markets or the economy. I say that because the "dots" [Fed officials' interest-rate-cut projections] don't show a dramatic interest in near-term rate cuts. Even though the Fed is watching the economy for any signs of weakness, it will likely take a lot of economic deterioration for the Fed the get more aggressive in supporting the economy. Practically, that means it is going to take a further substantial (maybe another 10% or more) decline in stocks (that will come as growth slows) before the Fed actively supports markets.

Put in market vernacular, yesterday's Fed decision reminded markets that the "Fed put" is still alive and the Fed is ready to use it. However, the level at which they will use it likely is still substantially lower from here.

Tom Essaye

Tariff Torrent Spells Trouble

North American Outlook BMO Capital Markets March 17: The shelf life of economic forecasts is now measured in days, even hours, due to the U.S. Administration's unpredictable trade policies. Uncertainty alone is harming investment, especially in Canada.

Though much less exposed to trade than Canada or Mexico, the U.S. economy will experience more than just flesh wounds in a trade war. We lowered our 2025 Q4/Q4 GDP growth outlook by 0.6 percentage points in response to the announced tariffs on Canada and Mexico, as well as the already imposed 20% levy on China. On an annual basis, U.S. GDP growth could slow to 1.8% in 2025 from 2.8% last year, marking the weakest performance in five years.

Tariffs, a regressive form of taxation, will disproportionately impact lower-income households. Widespread layoffs of federal government workers will exacerbate the pullback in spending. Business surveys indicate that investment has already been delayed due to trade uncertainty. The unemployment rate, which has steadied in recent months, is expected to rise to 4.5% by year end from 4.1% in February. It could rise further if substantial new levies are announced in early April.

Sal Gautieri

Sectors Poised to Rebound

Sector Watch CFRA March 17: There is an old saying that stocks tend to pop after a drop. Since the percentage of component stocks in the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 recently fell below their 30% lines in the sand that traditionally indicated an oversold condition, investors now have reason to believe that at least a near-term countertrend rally is at hand. What's more, should the correction have already run its course, history encourages investors to gravitate toward the three sectors and 10 subindustries that fell the furthest during this correction.

From Feb. 19 through March 13, the S&P 500 fell 10.1%, taking all sizes, styles, and sectors down with it, as well as 82% of its 127 subindustries. The sectors that held up the best included the traditional safe havens of consumer staples, healthcare, and utilities. The three worst performers were the communication services, consumer discretionary, and technology sectors.

Sam Stovall

How Bear Markets Differ

Special Update Crosscurrents March 17: The recent break of the long inclined trendline matches the 2022 bear, but the pattern for cumulative breadth is different. Today we would label breadth as significantly worse than the prior period, one of the main reasons why we have offered views that stocks could be hammered far more than in the 2022 bear.

We would expect to see fear show up readily on Nasdaq, as it is populated by more speculative issues trading at higher valuations. The Composite was down as much as 14.2% in only 15 days, yet our Nasdaq 5 Day Panic Ratio was about as subdued as we've ever seen in similar situations. Frankly, until the Panic Ratio reaches five [it is below four], we would consider odds for a bottom in place to be extremely remote.

Alan M. Newman

To be considered for this section, material, with the author's name and address, should be sent to MarketWatch@barrons.com.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Croatian share indices end mixed, Koncar leads decliners
data.description:
ZAGREB (Croatia), March 21 (SeeNews) - The main equity indices of the Zagreb Stock Exchange closed mixed amidst losses in electrical concern Koncar [ZSE:KOEI] and gains in food and pharmaceuticals producer Podravka [ZSE:PODR], bourse data showed on Friday.

The 19-share equity benchmark, the CROBEX, climbed 0.23% to 3,320.80 points. The narrower CROBEX10 index fell 0.11% to 2,087.65 points.

Koncar was the most traded company with a turnover of some 375,000 euro ($406,000), and the biggest daily decliner among the blue chips with its shared down 2.97% to 458 euro.

Podravka gained 0.70% to 143 euro in a turnover of 272,100 euro.

The bourse's regular turnover fell to 1.8 million euro from 6.3 million euro on Thursday, as trading with debt instruments dropped to some 200,000 euro from 5.5 million euro.

($ = 0.924 euro)

data.title: Stevie Wonder's Family Lists Longtime Calabasas Home for $15 Million — WSJ
data.description:
By Katherine Clarke

It was the acoustics that made Stevie Wonder fall in love with the house.

When the singer first saw the Los Angeles-area property about 25 years ago, his famed voice reverberated through the high-ceilinged home, according to his then-wife, clothing designer Kai Milla-Morris.

Wonder surprised her by buying the house in celebrity-studded Calabasas. "He says, 'I want to show you something,'" Milla-Morris recalled. "He had already purchased the house." Luckily, she loved the nearly 3-acre estate, and has lived there ever since; Wonder moved out after their 2012 divorce.

Now that their two sons are grown, Milla-Morris said, she is putting the property on the market for $15 million.

Located in a gated community, the six-bedroom property has 11,120 square feet of living space and a pool.

Milla-Morris married the "Isn't She Lovely?" singer in the Bahamas in 2000. Wonder, whose real name is Stevland Hardaway Morris, declined to comment. The 74-year-old musician has since remarried, but he and Milla-Morris remain close friends, she said.

Milla-Morris moved to Los Angeles from the East Coast after she married Wonder, and the couple lived at the Peninsula Hotel in Beverly Hills for close to a year while they searched for a home. They paid $2.85 million for the modern Mediterranean-style home in 2000, property records show.

"It just had this incredible, timeless architecture and breathtaking surroundings," Milla-Morris said. "It was on its own little hill and had lots of space."

Once they moved in, Milla-Morris said, there was always music around the house. "Steve, even the incredible musician as he is, he practiced every single day," she said. Her children also played the piano, and "the family was always having great jam sessions."

The home has a music area off the living room, where Milla-Morris keeps Wonder's rare Bösendorfer piano. It is not included in the sale. "We have several pianos throughout the house that the kids learned on," she said. "Once they learned how to play, then they started playing on the Bösendorfer."

The home, she said, has also been a gathering place for their wider, blended family, including Wonder's kids from other relationships.

Milla-Morris loved throwing epic birthday parties at the house. One Coachella-themed party had a Ferris wheel and food trucks. For another event, they had a harmonica-playing elephant; the same elephant starred in the movie "Ace Ventura: Pet Detective," she said.

Wonder and Milla-Morris didn't do much to the house, built in the 1990s, when they first moved in. "As a young bride and a mother, I had no interest in doing anything to the house," Milla-Morris said. "I had to let them be kids. They're going to mark the wall, put fingerprints everywhere. Just forget about it."

But her sons, Kailand and Mandla Morris, are now 19 and 23, and Milla-Morris spent at least $3 million upgrading the property during the pandemic. She remodeled the primary suite, dividing it into separate areas using a double-sided fireplace. The "vignettes," as she called them, allow the space to feel more intimate. She gut renovated the kitchen, opening it up to the rest of the living space and bringing in new finishes, like an oversize marble island crafted from two large pieces of book-matched marble. The property also has a formal dining room and a family room with a wine-storage area and bar.

Milla-Morris said she feels it is time to sell the property, which will also be listed as a rental for $65,000 a month.

"It's very big," she said. "I feel like this is the right time to venture out into a new space."

The property is listed by Branden Williams, Rayni Williams, Dennis DeWalt and Brandon Meneses of the Beverly Hills Estates.

Write to Katherine Clarke at Katherine.Clarke@wsj.com

data.title: S&P/TSX Composite Index Ends the Week 1.69% Higher at 24968.49 — Data Talk
data.description:
The S&P/TSX Composite Index is up 415.09 points or 1.69% this week to 24968.49

  • Largest one-week point and percentage gain since the week ending Nov 22, 2024
  • Snaps a two-week losing streak
  • Today it is down 91.75 points or 0.37%
  • Largest one-day point and percentage decline since Thursday, March 13, 2025

  • Down 100.72 points or 0.40% over the last two trading days

  • Off 3.25% from its 52-week high of 25808.25 hit Thursday, Jan. 30, 2025

data.title: Utilities Down, But not by Much, on Safe-Haven Demand — Utilities Roundup
data.description:
Shares of power producers fell, finishing flat on the week, amid defensive demand.

The utilities industry group has held up better than the broad market during the recent correction, as it stands to gain from the slide in Treasury yields and an appetite for safe havens.

Investors are retreating from cyclical sectors due to the growing uncertainty about the outlook for growth and inflation cited by global central banks, including the Federal Reserve.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

Levi & Korsinsky Announces the Filing of a Securities Class Action on Behalf of ModivCare Inc. (MODV) Shareholders
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / March 21, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your ModivCare Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/modivcare-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=137906&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

<img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/api/iframe.ashx?releaseid=1003885&url=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fembed%2frKM2z0RiBKo" width="0"/></div></div></div><p><strong>THE LAWSUIT:</strong> A class action securities lawsuit was filed against ModivCare Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between November 3, 2022 and September 15, 2024.</p><p><strong>CASE DETAILS:</strong> According to the filed complaint, defendants made false statements and/or concealed that certain contracts used in ModivCare's NEMT segment caused the Company's free cash flow to deteriorate and as a result, (1) contract renegotiations and pricing accommodations negatively impacted the Company's adjusted EBITDA; (2) the Company had insufficient liquidity; and (3) defendants' positive statements about the Company's business, operations, and prospects were materially misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis.</p><p><strong>WHAT'S NEXT?</strong> If you suffered a loss in ModivCare stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to <a href="https://pr.report/8l7n" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/pslra-1/modivcare-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=137906&wire=1</a> to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. <strong>There is no cost or obligation to participate.</strong></p><p><strong>WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY:</strong> Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.</p><p><strong>CONTACT:</strong><br/>Levi & Korsinsky, LLP<br/>Joseph E. Levi, Esq.<br/>Ed Korsinsky, Esq.<br/>33 Whitehall Street, 17th Floor<br/>New York, NY 10004<br/><a href="mailto:jlevi@levikorsinsky.com" rel="nofollow">jlevi@levikorsinsky.com</a><br/>Tel: (212) 363-7500<br/>Fax: (212) 363-7171<br/><a href="https://pr.report/8l7o" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/</a></p><p><strong>SOURCE:</strong> Levi & Korsinsky, LLP</p><br/><br/>View the original <a href="https://www.accessnewswire.com/newsroom/en/business-and-professional-services/levi-and-korsinsky-announces-the-filing-of-a-securities-class-ac-1003885">press release</a> on ACCESS Newswire<br/><br/><img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/img.ashx?id=1003885" width="0"/></body></html>
